Bio Kit

Ready-to-use biographies

Copy any version below for podcasts, conference programs, paper author bios, LinkedIn, or press releases. All text is pre-formatted and production-ready.

Aaron Snowberger
에런 스노우버거 · Dr.
Position Lecturer, JBNU
Location Jeonju, South Korea
Ph.D. ICE · Computer Vision/AI
MFA Media Design
Research Physical AI · CV · EdTech
Pronouns he / him
Website aaron.kr
Email aaron@aaronsnowberger.com

Dr. Aaron Snowberger is an American AI researcher and computer science educator at Jeonbuk National University in South Korea. His research focuses on Physical AI, computer vision, and handwritten character recognition — with a particular interest in bridging East–West perspectives in technology education.

~50 words · Short

Dr. Aaron Snowberger is an American AI researcher and computer science educator based in Jeonju, South Korea, where he lectures at Jeonbuk National University. He holds a Ph.D. in Information and Communications Engineering (specializing in Computer Vision/AI) and an MFA in Media Design.

His research sits at the intersection of Physical AI, computer vision, and CS education — with a focus on handwritten character recognition, digital twin simulation, and cross-cultural technology pedagogy. He is a co-founding member of the Korea Society for Physical AI (KSPAI).

~90 words · Medium

Dr. Aaron Snowberger (에런 스노우버거) is an American AI researcher, computer science educator, and multimedia designer based in Jeonju, South Korea. He lectures in computer science, programming, IoT, and circuits across multiple Korean universities, with his primary affiliation at Jeonbuk National University (JBNU).

He holds a Ph.D. in Information and Communications Engineering — specializing in Computer Vision and AI — alongside an MFA in Media Design and a B.S. in Computer Science. His research focuses on Physical AI, handwritten character recognition (including an original Hangul pangram dataset), and the application of digital twin simulation in education.

A co-founding member and webmaster of the Korea Society for Physical AI (KSPAI), Aaron's work bridges technical rigor with pedagogical innovation, connecting East–West perspectives on AI methodology and CS education. He is an ordained deacon, worship leader, bass guitarist, and fitness enthusiast — a builder and tinkerer who came to academia by way of graphic design and English magazine publishing in Korea.

~175 words · Long

Dr. Aaron Snowberger is a lecturer in Computer Science at Jeonbuk National University, South Korea, and a co-founding member of the Korea Society for Physical AI (KSPAI). He received his Ph.D. in Information and Communications Engineering from [University Name], specializing in Computer Vision and AI, and holds an MFA in Media Design and a B.S. in Computer Science.

His research interests include Physical AI methodology, handwritten character recognition (with focus on Hangul, Cherokee, and Manchu scripts), digital twin simulation in education, and cross-cultural technology perception. He is currently developing an original Hangul pangram dataset targeting publication in Data in Brief and IEEE Access.

~110 words · Academic / Paper

Dr. Aaron Snowberger is an AI researcher, CS educator, and cross-cultural technologist who has spent over a decade living and working in South Korea. Originally from the United States, Aaron brings a rare East–West perspective to conversations about AI education, physical AI, and the future of human–machine collaboration.

He teaches computer science and AI at Jeonbuk National University, co-founded the Korea Society for Physical AI, and holds a Ph.D. in Computer Vision/AI alongside an MFA in Media Design. Whether he's wiring IoT circuits, researching handwritten script recognition, or leading university lectures in Korean, Aaron's work lives at the creative edge of technology and education.

~120 words · Speaker / Podcast
